title: lolium temulentum subsp. robustum
intro: Lolium temulentum subsp. robustum, also known as robust false oats, is a subspecies of Lolium temulentum, a plant of the grass family Poaceae, that contains a toxic alkaloid called lysergic acid amide. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Lolium temulentum subsp. robustum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties and potential interactions with other medications.
